user_query:
I'm meeting with Northwind Analytics next week. Please create a pre-meeting intelligence brief strictly from the documents in input/ and do not use any external sources.

Read these files from input/:
- input/company_profile.json
- input/trajectory.json
- input/executive_maya_patel.txt
- input/job_postings.csv
- input/competitors.yaml
- input/reviews.json
- input/recent_news.jsonl
- input/website_updates.md

Deliverables:
1) Write a narrative brief to output/brief.md with the following exact section headings, in this order:
- Business Fundamentals
- Company Trajectory
- Executive / Contact Background
- Industry Signals
- Recent Activity (Last 90 Days)
- Fit Assessment
- Conversation Angles

End the brief with a one-line Fit Assessment of Strong/Moderate/Weak and 2–3 specific Conversation Angles tied to the evidence. After key claims, cite your source by filename in square brackets, e.g., [source: input/company_profile.json]. Do not cite any sources outside input/.

2) Write a fact summary to output/extracts.json with this JSON schema:
{
  "company_name": string,
  "products": [string],
  "target_customers": [string],
  "executive_contact": {"name": string, "role": string},
  "key_events_last_12_months": [ { "date": "YYYY-MM-DD", "type": "funding|partnership|acquisition|product_update|news", "title": string } ],
  "competitors": [string],
  "review_pain_points": [string],
  "job_signals": [string],
  "fit_assessment": "Strong|Moderate|Weak",
  "conversation_angles": [string]
}

Standards: verify before asserting; if something isn't in the inputs, omit it. Keep the brief readable and business-relevant. Ensure the Recent Activity section emphasizes items dated within the last 90 days from the provided files.
